subject: First Year Algebra
instructions: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Let \(G\) be a finite \(p\)-group and suppose that \(G\) acts on a finite set \(S\). Denote by \(S^G\) the subset of \(S\) fixed by every element of \(G\). Show that \(|S| \equiv |S^G| \pmod p\), where \(|A|\) denotes the cardinality of the set \(A\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: How many elements of \(S_6\) have order 2?

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: How many elements of \(A_6\) have order 2?

problems.text: Let \(G\) be a finite group, and suppose \(d\) is an integer dividing the order of \(G\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Show the number of elements of order \(d\) is divisible by \(\phi(d)\) (Euler \(\phi\)-function).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Give an example in which the number of elements of order \(d\) is zero.

subparts.label: (iii)
subparts.text: Give an example in which the number of elements of order \(d\) is strictly larger than \(\phi(d)\).

problems.text: Show that a group of order \(385 = 5 \cdot 7 \cdot 11\) has a normal cyclic subgroup of order 77.

Let \(G\) be a group that is the internal direct product of two characteristic subgroups \(H\) and \(K\). Show that \(\operatorname{Aut}(G) \simeq \operatorname{Aut}(H) \times \operatorname{Aut}(K)\). Can the hypothesis that \(H\) and \(K\) are characteristic be omitted?
Let \(p\) be a prime and \(n \geq 3\) an integer. Show that there exist nonabelian groups of order \(p^n\) with a cyclic subgroup of index \(p\).
